data.title: LG Display GAAP EPS of KRW891B, revenue of KRW5.59T
data.description:
• LG Display press release (NYSE:LPL): Q2 GAAP EPS of KRW891B.
  • Revenue of KRW5.59T (-16.7% Y/Y).

data.title: Google Cloud was the star in Alphabet's Q2 report - analysts
data.description:
Google parent Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) reported second-quarter earnings on Wednesday after the bell that beat expectations easily, with another double-digit gain for revenues—and featured a sharp increase to its spending forecast.

Revenue breakdown: Google Search and other, $54.19B (up 11.7%); YouTube ads, $9.8B (up 13.1%); Google Network, $7.35B (down 1.2%); Google subscriptions, platforms and devices, $11.2B (up 20.3%); Google Cloud, $13.6B (up 31.7%); Other Bets, $373M (up 2.2%).

FY capex was hiked to $85B vs. a previous forecast for $75B (Bloomberg estimates $73.3B).

Shares of the company are up 3.6% in premarket trading on Thursday.

Word on the Street

Needham (PT hiked by $10 to $220; rating "buy") The research firm noted in the tech giant's results that demand for its cloud services continued to outpace capacity; Gemini AI Adoption grew35x y/y in 2Q25, and is now used by >85,000 enterprises; YouTube's ad revs grew by 13% y/y, driven by performance advertisers followed by brand advertisers; and Search revs increased 12% y/y driven by GenAI, as well as strength in the financial services and retail verticals, among other things.

"We like GOOGL's strong strategic position as #1 in Search, #1 in Streaming (YouTube), #2 in Mobile (Android), #3 in Cloud; and #1 in Autonomous driving (Waymo). LLMs make data more valuable and GOOGL's data is best in class," Needham said.

Evercore ISI (PT hiked by $35 to $240; rating "outperform") "Google Cloud absolutely crushed it," the research firm said.

"Even with expectations rising into the print, revenue and operating oncome results came in better than expected, and fundamental trends were nicely positive – organic revenue growth acceleration and operating margin expansion.Ad revenue growth trends were very consistent & positive – Search & YouTube growth both accelerated on a reported basis. Operating margin remained at a 12-year high at 34%  thanks to scale and compensation discipline and despite accelerating depreciation growth," Evercore said.

Morgan Stanley (PT hiked by $5 to $210; rating "overweight") "Search, YouTube and Google Cloud all accelerated as GenAI-enabled innovation is driving faster growth," the research firm said.

They noted that the company raised its 2025 capex guidance to $85B from $75B, and believe part of this could be funded by the potential ~$25B cash flow tax benefit in 2025 from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act. "This investment step up is an important factor we think investors should keep in mind heading into META and AMZN earnings next week given the similar investment-driven tax benefits they are likely to receive too."

Wells Fargo (PT hiked by $7 to $184; rating "equal weight") "Cloud outperformance in qtr driven by core cloud, not AI, as supply constraints will continue to limit the utilization of the robust reported backlog," the research firm said and noted that cloud acceleration stands out as a positive.

"See 2Q print as enough given YTD share underperformance (-21% vs. META). Ads rather underwhelming given bullish investor expects... See likely benign search case remedy decision, expected 1H Aug, as next catalyst," Wells Fargo said.

Wedbush (PT hiked by $25 to $225; rating "outperform") "Alphabet reported strong 2Q results across all segments. Management commentary should alleviate investor caution around the perceived risks of generative AI on the Search business," the research firm said.

On Cloud, they noted that continued momentum was led by AI infrastructure, genAI solutions, and core GCP products. Cloud business continues to observe excess demand relative to capacity, due to which the company, now plans to spend $85B in capex this year, raised from $75B guidance provided last quarter. Cloud demand should start to alleviate in the back half of this year and into 2026. 

Morningstar (PT $237, rating 4 stars out of 5) "Alphabet's strong second-quarter results, along with management commentary on solid artificial intelligence adoption and usage further strengthen our confidence in the firm's competitive positioning in AI and the resilience of its core Search business," the research firm said.

"We were encouraged to see the continued traction of AI search features... On the advertising front, we like Alphabet's integration and use of AI-enabled tools to drive better returns for its advertisers... Moving to Google Cloud, the segment's acceleration to 32% growth from the 28% growth it saw last quarter is a clear indication of strong AI-led public cloud infrastructure demand," Morningstar said.

Seeking Alpha analysts

Ahan Vashi (rating "buy"): "Alphabet Inc. delivered strong Q2 2025 results, with robust growth across Search, YouTube, and Cloud, driven by AI innovation. Despite increased AI-driven CAPEX pressuring free cash flow, Alphabet remains fundamentally healthy and trades at a discount to big tech peers. My valuation model shows GOOGL stock is about 15% overvalued absolutely, but a 5-year CAGR of 11.5% justifies a continued Buy rating."

Agar Capital (rating "strong buy"): "Alphabet Inc. delivered a strong quarter, beating expectations on all fronts, yet the market fixates on $85B capex and misses the underlying quality and growth. I see Alphabet as a rare value: robust cash flow, accelerating cloud and YouTube profitability, and a fortress balance sheet, all trading at a discount. The market's anxiety over high capex is misplaced—these are strategic AI infrastructure investments, not reckless spending, and protect long-term competitiveness."

Hunting Alpha (rating "buy"): "Alphabet's Q2 FY25 results reinforce my bullish stance, driven by AI-led growth in Search that is leading to strong user engagement and importantly, higher ad conversions. Google Cloud is showing signs of accelerated growth, evidenced by a surge in high-value deals and a robust backlog, supporting future revenue expansion. GOOGL stock remains undervalued versus peers and its own historical multiples."

data.title: Union Pacific, Norfolk Southern in advanced merger talks
data.description:
Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) and Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) confirmed that the railroad operators are in advanced talks regarding a potential business combination. Union Pacific fell 2.8% in pre-market trading, while Norfolk Southern rose 3.5%.

There's no assurance that any agreement for a transaction will be reached or the terms of any such transaction, the companies said in a statement on Thursday. The railroad operators don't intend to make additional comments or provide an update until they determine that disclosure is required.

The confirmation of talks comes after the WSJ reported a week ago that Union Pacific (NYSE:UNP) was exporing a deal for Norfolk Southern (NYSE:NSC) that could create a $200B U.S. coast-to-coast rail network.

data.title: Ladder Capital Q2 earnings beat by a penny as expenses pull back
data.description:
Ladder Capital (NYSE:LADR) on Thursday turned in second-quarter earnings that slightly topped the average analyst estimate as the mortgage REIT recorded lower expenses.

Q2 distributable EPS of $0.23, slightly above the $0.22 consensus, rose from $0.20 in the prior quarter, but slid from $0.31 a year ago.

LADR shares gained 0.9% in premarket trading.

Q2 net interest income was $21.5M in Q1, up from $20.3M in Q1 and down from $34.2M in the year-ago period.

Real estate operating income advanced to $25.8M in Q1 from $21.8M in Q1 and edged down from $26.1M in 2024's Q2.

Total costs and operating expenses of $35.5M compared with $40.6M in both Q1 2025 and Q2 2024.

Conference call at 10:00 a.m. ET.

data.title: Textron posts higher Q2 revenue, raises 2025 cash outlook on aviation, defense growth
data.description:
Textron (NYSE:TXT) reported second-quarter earnings on Thursday that beat Wall Street estimates for both revenue and adjusted profit, driven by gains in its aviation and defense segments. Shares were little changed in premarket trading.

The industrial conglomerate posted revenue of $3.72 billion for the quarter ended June 28, up 5.4% year-over-year and above the consensus estimate of $3.65 billion. Adjusted earnings per share came in at $1.55, up slightly from $1.54 a year earlier and topping the $1.45 expected by analysts.

The company's GAAP earnings per share held steady at $1.35.

Chairman and Chief Executive Scott Donnelly highlighted growth in Textron Aviation and Bell, noting that operations benefited from increased jet deliveries and momentum in Bell’s FLRAA program, now designated the MV-75.

“We saw revenue growth in both our commercial aircraft and helicopter businesses,” Donnelly said in a statement, adding that manufacturing improvements at Textron Aviation supported the performance.

Segment highlights

  • Textron Aviation delivered 49 jets in the quarter, up from 42 a year ago. Segment revenue rose to $1.52 billion. However, segment profit declined to $180 million from $195 million due to less favorable product mix and higher warranty costs.
  • Bell revenue surged 28% to slightly more than $1 billion, driven by higher military aircraft volume, particularly from the MV-75 program. Segment profit dipped slightly to $80 million as R&D expenses increased.
  • Textron Systems posted stable revenue of $321 million and a 14% rise in profit to $40 million, aided by lower administrative expenses.
  • Industrial revenue fell to $839 million, hurt by the sale of the Powersports business and lower vehicle volumes. Still, segment profit improved to $54 million on cost reductions tied to restructuring efforts.
  • Textron eAviation, the company’s electric aviation unit, reported a loss of $16 million on $8 million in revenue.

Cash flow, capital returns

Textron (NYSE:TXT) generated $336 million in manufacturing cash flow before pension contributions during the quarter, up from $320 million a year ago. The company returned $214 million to shareholders via share repurchases in the quarter and has bought back $429 million in stock year to date.

Bolstered by its strong performance and new U.S. tax legislation, Textron (TXT) raised its 2025 manufacturing cash flow outlook to a range of $900 million to $1.0 billion, up $100 million from prior guidance.

Outlook

Textron reiterated its full-year 2025 EPS guidance of $5.19 to $5.39 on a GAAP basis and $6.00 to $6.20 on an adjusted basis.

Backlogs remained robust across its key businesses, with Textron Aviation’s backlog totaling $7.85 billion and Bell’s at $6.9 billion at quarter-end, the company said.

data.title: Senmiao Technology announces 1-for-10 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Senmiao Technology (NASDAQ:AIHS) expects to implement a 1-for-10 reverse stock split on its common stock, par value $0.0001 per share effective Tuesday, July 29, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: HEICO acquires avionics company Gables Engineering
data.description:
• HEICO Corporation (NYSE:HEI) said on Thursday that its Electronic Technologies Group has acquired Gables Engineering for cash at closing. Further transaction terms and financial details were not disclosed.
  • HEICO stated that it expects the acquisition to be accretive to its earnings within a year following the purchase.
  • Gables Engineering is a designer and manufacturer of advanced avionics controls, including navigation, audio, surveillance, and communication panels for a wide range of aircraft.

data.title: Sunrun, Tesla partner to launch home energy plan in Texas
data.description:
Sunrun (NASDAQ:RUN) +4.8% pre-market Thursday after unveiling a new home energy plan in partnership with Tesla (TSLA) designed for Sunrun Flex customers in Texas, which it said will maximize solar production, battery backup power for outages, and cost predictability.

The Tesla Electric + Sunrun Flex plan will combine Sunrun's (NASDAQ:RUN) solar and storage subscription service with a customized Tesla Electric (TSLA) retail plan, offering fixed electricity rates and competitive sellback rates for excess solar energy returned to the grid, Sunrun said.

The partnership aims to address challenges in the Texas electricity market, where consumers face a crowded array of retail providers and complex rate structures that are often not optimized for solar and storage systems.

"This partnership creates the most compelling and comprehensive home energy solution on the market for Texas consumers," Sunrun (RUN) CEO Mary Powell said.

data.title: AmpliTech receives $3M in new orders beyond $40M LOI
data.description:
• AmpliTech (NASDAQ:AMPG) announced on Thursday the receipt of approximately $5M in follow-on orders from a tier-1 North American mobile network operator. 
  • Of the $5M in new bookings, $3M represent brand new orders above and beyond the previously disclosed $40M Letter of Intent (LOI), reflecting growing demand for AmpliTech’s 5G Open RAN radio products. 
  • The remaining $2 million are additional quantities within the scope of the original LOI. The increased demand follows successful deployment and performance of configurations shipped during the second quarter of 2025.
  • From July 1 through July 24, AmpliTech has recorded company-wide bookings exceeding $7 million.  
  • AmpliTech (NASDAQ:AMPG) shares were up more than 6% in premarket trading.

data.title: 5 stocks to watch on Thursday: HON, LVS, TMUS, AVGO and STM
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed on Thursday, as investors digested Alphabet and Tesla earnings, while markets also assessed reports of President Donald Trump's plans to visit the Federal Reserve. Below are five stocks to keep an eye on:

Honeywell International (HON) shares fell 3% in premarket trade after the industrial conglomerate reported contracting margins and sluggish performance in key automation segments for the second quarter. Adjusted earnings were $2.75 a share, and revenue rose 8% to $10.4 billion, both beating expectations. 

Las Vegas Sands (LVS) stock rose 6.2% before the opening bell as the casino operator posted a strong Q2 earnings report. The firm's revenue was up 15.2% to $3.18 billion, while EPS came in at $0.79 vs. $0.53 consensus. 

T-Mobile US (TMUS) shares rose 4% in premarket trade after the company blew past expectations by topping the consensus estimate for revenue, profit, key subscriber metrics, and increased forecast. In 2025, the company now expects postpaid net customer additions of 6.1M to 6.4M (est. 5.94M), up from 5.5M to 6M.

Broadcom (AVGO) stock will be monitored after the Cloud Infrastructure Service Providers in Europe filed an appeal before the European General Court challenging the European Commission’s decision to approve Broadcom's (AVGO) acquisition of VMware.

STMicroelectronics N.V. (STM) shares were down 14% in premarket trade after the company missed second-quarter EPS estimates and also reported an operating loss due to restructuring charges. For Q3, the firm expects net revenues to be $3.17 billion at midpoint, versus a consensus of $3.18 billion.
